ingredients.prep: or enough to fully cover peanuts by 2–3 inches
plus more to taste (salt level varies by preference)

steps.text: Rinse peanuts: Rinse raw peanuts thoroughly in cool water to remove dirt. Discard any damaged shells.
Start the boil: Place peanuts in a large stockpot. Add water to cover by 2–3 inches. Add salt and any optional seasonings (Cajun seasoning, cayenne, garlic/onion powder, bay leaves).
Boil then simmer: Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a steady simmer. Peanuts will float—stir occasionally and add water as needed to keep them covered.

Cook until tender: Simmer until peanuts are soft and the shells feel pliable. This can take 2–4+ hours depending on peanut freshness. Taste a peanut periodically; it should be fully tender and salty throughout.
Adjust seasoning: If you want them saltier, add a little more salt and simmer 15–20 minutes, then re-taste.
Soak for more flavor: Turn off heat and let peanuts soak in the brine 30–60 minutes (or longer) to absorb more salt and seasoning.
Serve and store: Serve warm with some brine. Refrigerate leftovers in a bit of brine for best texture; reheat gently.
